name: Senda Dolça Trail (PR-CV 109)
name_original: Senda Dolza (PR-CV 109) — Circular a la Serra de Crevillent
description: Hike the moderate 8.4-mile Senda Dolza (PR-CV 109) in the Serra de Crevillent, gaining 2008 feet to panoramic views from Pico La Vella.
region_intro: Hiking around Crevillent offers a diverse range of landscapes, from rugged mountains to serene wetlands and a picturesque reservoir. The region is characterized by the Sierra de Crevillent, featuring peaks around 800 meters that provide extensive views. Additionally, the area includes the significant wetlands of El Hondo Natural Park and the tranquil Embalse de Crevillent. These varied environments create a rich backdrop for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Crevillent The most popular hiking route is Green Route: El Rincón and El Saladar Lagoons, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 6 minutes to complete. This easy route is located within El Hondo Natural Park, offering panoramic lagoon views and opportunities for birdwatching. Another top favourite among local hikers is Els Anouers (PR-CV 110) — Picacho circular desde el Área Recreativa de San Cayetano, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.8 km) path. This trail in the Sierra de Crevillent provides panoramic views and unique rock formations. Local hikers also love the Senda Dolça Trail (PR-CV 109), an 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail leading through the Serra de Crevillent, often completed in about 4 hours 37 minutes. This moderate route ascends to Pico La Vella, offering expansive views. Hiking around Crevillent is defined by rugged mountains, extensive wetlands, and a scenic reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The routes in Crevillent are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 380 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Crevillent's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Crevillent?
A: Crevillent offers a wide selection of hiking experiences, with over 60 distinct trails. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Crevillent?
A: Hiking in Crevillent provides a diverse range of landscapes. You can explore the rugged peaks and valleys of the Sierra de Crevillent, the serene wetlands of El Hondo Natural Park, and the tranquil setting around the Embalse de Crevillent reservoir. The region is known for its varied ecosystems and panoramic views.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Crevillent?
A: Yes, Crevillent has 13 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316845595/green-route-el-rincon-and-el-saladar-lagoons" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green Route: El Rincón and El Saladar Lagoons&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.4 km route within El Hondo Natural Park, offering beautiful lagoon views and birdwatching opportunities.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Crevillent?
A: Yes, many trails in Crevillent are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316322061/els-anouers-pr-cv-110-picacho-circular-desde-el-area-recreativa-de-san-cayetano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Anouers (PR-CV 110) — Picacho circular&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.8 km loop offering panoramic views in the Sierra de Crevillent. Another option is the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316945235/pena-negra-archaeological-site-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra Archaeological Site Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Crevillent?
A: The hiking routes in Crevillent are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 380 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from mountain views to wetland serenity, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls or natural pools near Crevillent?
A: While large waterfalls are not a primary feature, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2440117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elche Reservoir and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; nearby. Additionally, the Sierra de Crevillent is home to natural pools like the 'Charca de los Sapos' (pond of the toads), offering refreshing spots along some routes. The northern part of the Embalse de Crevillent also provides opportunities for swimming.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting on the trails?
A: Absolutely. El Hondo Natural Park is a significant wetland and a haven for birdwatching, offering panoramic lagoon views and a rich ecosystem. In the Sierra de Crevillent, you might spot local wildlife such as wild goats and eagles, especially on routes like the PR-CV 104.

Q: Are there any historical or archaeological sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, Crevillent offers a blend of nature and history. You can hike to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316945235/pena-negra-archaeological-site-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, also known as the city of Herna, which provides insights into ancient settlements and good views. The Cueva de la Serreta also contains ancient cave paintings and ruins, adding a historical dimension to your exploration.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Crevillent dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in Crevillent are suitable for dogs, especially those in natural areas like the Sierra de Crevillent and around the Embalse de Crevillent. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and check specific trail regulations or local signage for any restrictions, particularly in protected areas like El Hondo Natural Park.

Q: Which trails offer the best panoramic views in Crevillent?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, head to the Sierra de Crevillent. The four highest peaks, all around 800 meters, offer 360-degree vistas that can extend as far as the Sierra de la Sagra on clear days. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868817898/senda-dolca-trail-pr-cv-109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda Dolça Trail (PR-CV 109)&lt;/a&gt;, which ascends to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico La Vella&lt;/a&gt;, are excellent for expansive views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Crevillent?
A: The best time to hike in Crevillent is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable if visiting during June to September. Spring brings wildflowers, while autumn offers pleasant temperatures and clear skies.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Crevillent, especially during summer?
A: For hiking in Crevillent, especially during summer, lightweight, breathable clothing is essential. Opt for moisture-wicking fabrics, a wide-brimmed hat, and sunglasses. Always carry plenty of water, as temperatures can be high. In cooler months, layers are advisable, as temperatures can vary between day and night and at different elevations.
tips: The La Vella peak is part of the Sierra de Crevillent and offers wonderful views. You reach the top along the marked path in good condition and without too much technical difficulty. It is recommended to bring plenty of water because there are no sources on the way.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Senda Dolza (PR-CV 109)?
faqs.answer: The Senda Dolza (PR-CV 109) is approximately 13.5 kilometers (8 miles) long. Most hikers complete the route in about 4 to 5 hours, depending on their pace and stops for views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail features a varied terrain, including accessible paths, footpaths, mountain hiking paths, and unpaved sections. You'll encounter natural ground, some alpine environments, and stretches along ramblas (dry riverbeds). Be aware that some parts, especially near Pico La Vella, require sure-footedness.

faqs.question: Where does the Senda Dolza (PR-CV 109) trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Senda Dolza (PR-CV 109) is a circular route that typically begins and ends at Els Pontets, an area known for its old aqueduct. Parking is generally available near this starting point, but it's always advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is the Senda Dolza (PR-CV 109) suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as **moderate**. While it requires good fitness and some sure-footedness, the paths are mostly accessible. It's not ideal for absolute beginners due to its length and elevation gain, but those with some hiking experience should find it manageable. Proper planning is recommended due to reported deficiencies in signage.

faqs.question: What viewpoints and landmarks can I expect to see along the Senda Dolza?
faqs.answer: The highlight of the route is the ascent to Pico La Vella, the highest point in the Serra de Crevillent, offering magnificent panoramic views over the Bajo Vinalopó region, the Crevillent reservoir, and surrounding sierras. You'll also pass by ancient constructions like the protohistoric site of Herma or Peña Negra, and old refuge buildings, adding a historical dimension to the scenic beauty.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike the Senda Dolza are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, making the hike more challenging, and there are no water sources along the way. Winter can also be pleasant, but always check weather conditions before heading out.

faqs.question: Is the Senda Dolza (PR-CV 109) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the varied terrain and potential for wildlife. Remember to carry plenty of water for both yourself and your dog, as there are no water sources on the trail.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Serra de Crevillent?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Senda Dolza (PR-CV 109) or access the Serra de Crevillent. It is a publicly accessible trail.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other routes in the Serra de Crevillent?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Senda Dolza (PR-CV 109) is part of the broader network of trails in the region. It fully covers the 'Sierra de Crevillente' trail and also intersects with significant portions of 'Sierra de Crevillent' and 'El Camí IT5TR3 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana'.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike, especially regarding water?
faqs.answer: It is crucial to carry plenty of water, as there are no natural water sources available along the entire route. Additionally, bring appropriate hiking footwear for varied terrain, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), snacks, and a map or GPS device, as some trail signage has been reported as deficient.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends at Els Pontets, which is close to Crevillent. In Crevillent itself, you can find various amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel before or after your hike.
